machinery_genes.note: C. rodentium LEE ORF (rCR3) named escC with predicted function 'Type III secretion' in Table 3 of Deng et al. 2001. SctC outer-membrane secretin role assigned from the apparatus brief vocabulary.
C. rodentium LEE ORF (rCR5) named escJ, function 'Type III secretion' (Deng 2001, Table 3). SctJ inner-ring lipoprotein role from brief vocabulary.
C. rodentium LEE ORF (rCR9) named escD, function 'Type III secretion' (Deng 2001, Table 3). SctD inner MS-ring role from brief vocabulary.
C. rodentium LEE ORF (CR6) named escR, function 'Type III secretion' (Deng 2001, Table 3). SctR export-gate (export-apparatus inner-membrane) role from brief vocabulary.
C. rodentium LEE ORF (CR7) named escS, function 'Type III secretion' (Deng 2001, Table 3). SctS export-gate role from brief vocabulary.
C. rodentium LEE ORF (CR8) named escT, function 'Type III secretion' (Deng 2001, Table 3). SctT export-gate role from brief vocabulary.
C. rodentium LEE ORF (CR9 in Table 3) named escU, function 'Type III secretion' (Deng 2001). SctU export-gate autoprotease/switch role from brief vocabulary.

C. rodentium LEE ORF (CR13) named escV, function 'Type III secretion' (Deng 2001, Table 3). SctV major export-apparatus ring role from brief vocabulary; UniProt D2TKF6 annotates C. rodentium ICC168 EscV as 'T3SS structural protein EscV'.

machinery_genes.quote: The escN gene encodes an ATPase essential for type III secretion
machinery_genes.note: Named as the T3SS ATPase directly in C. rodentium (Deng et al. 2010 secretome). Also listed as escN 'Type III secretion' (CR14) in Deng 2001 Table 3.

machinery_genes.role: needle subunit (filament of the secretion needle)
machinery_genes.quote: the needle protein EscF and the putative inner rod component rOrf8 are also secreted by the LEE-encoded T3SS
machinery_genes.note: Named as the needle protein of the C. rodentium LEE-encoded T3SS (Deng 2010 secretome). Deng 2001 (Table 3, CR28) also names escF 'Type III secretion' and the text notes its Yersinia homolog forms the needle-like structure of the secretion apparatus.

machinery_genes.quote: the needle protein EscF and the putative inner rod component rOrf8 are also secreted by the LEE-encoded T3SS
machinery_genes.note: rOrf8 = EscI-family inner rod of the C. rodentium LEE T3SS (Deng 2010 secretome). LEE-conserved ORF; SctI/EscI inner-rod role from brief vocabulary.

C. rodentium LEE ORF (CR17) named sepQ, function 'Type III secretion' (Deng 2001, Table 3). SepQ is the EscQ ortholog (SctQ cytoplasmic C-ring/sorting platform) per brief vocabulary; not a transcriptional regulator.

C. rodentium LEE ORF (rCR4) named sepD, function 'Type III secretion' (Deng 2001, Table 3). SepD acts with SepL as the secretion-order switch controlling translocator-vs-effector export; an apparatus-associated secretion component, not a transcriptional regulator. Lower-confidence as a structural machinery part (functions in secretion control).

C. rodentium LEE ORF (CR23) named sepL, function 'Type III secretion' (Deng 2001, Table 3). SepL is the SctW-family gatekeeper (counterpart of YopN/TyeA, MxiC, InvE) that regulates the translocator-to-effector secretion switch; apparatus-associated, not a transcriptional regulator.

C. rodentium LEE ORF (rCR7) named sepZ, function 'Type III secretion' (Deng 2001, Table 3). SepZ (EspZ locus region / Sep designation = 'secretion of E. coli proteins') is a LEE secretion-essential gene retained where a clear Yersinia structural homolog was lacking; conservative role, lower-confidence family assignment.

machinery_genes.quote: the 3 translocators, EspA, EspD, and EspB
machinery_genes.note: EspA forms the filamentous sheath extending the needle to the host cell; classed as apparatus (translocon) per the brief, despite being secreted. Confirmed translocator in C. rodentium secretome (Deng 2010).

machinery_genes.quote: the 3 translocators, EspA, EspD, and EspB
machinery_genes.note: EspD is a hydrophobic translocator forming the host-membrane pore with EspB; apparatus (translocon) per the brief, not an effector. Confirmed in C. rodentium secretome (Deng 2010).

machinery_genes.quote: the 3 translocators, EspA, EspD, and EspB
machinery_genes.note: EspB is a translocator forming the host-membrane pore with EspD; apparatus (translocon) per the brief. (EspB also has effector-like activities once injected, but it is named here as a translocator.) Confirmed in C. rodentium secretome (Deng 2010).

notes: C. rodentium ICC168 carries a single, well-characterised T3SS: the LEE (locus of enterocyte effacement) injectisome, the archetypal A/E (attaching-and-effacing) secretion system shared with EPEC/EHEC. The genome paper (Petty et al. 2010, PMID 19897651) states the genome has 10 prophage-like regions and 17 genomic islands INCLUDING the LEE, and that all 29 effectors (LEE- and non-LEE/prophage-encoded) are injected by this one LEE T3SS. The benchmark instance label 'prophage_CRP2' tags the genomic neighbourhood of this instance's curated effectors (ROD_06241, ROD_06251, ROD_07221, which are prophage/MGE-associated non-LEE effectors); there is NO separate prophage-encoded T3SS apparatus in this genome. Per the brief's separated-paper rule, the apparatus for these prophage effectors is the LEE Esc/Sep machinery, curated here from C. rodentium-specific literature. Source of record for gene naming is Deng et al. 2001 (Infect Immun), the full C. rodentium LEE sequence: its Table 3 names each apparatus ORF (escC, escD, escJ, escN, escR, escS, escT, escU, escV, escF, sepD, sepL, sepQ, sepZ) with predicted function 'Type III secretion', and the text confirms EscF forms the needle and EspA/B/D are secreted translocators. Deng et al. 2010 (J Biol Chem secretome, PMID 20034934) adds direct functional naming: EscN = ATPase, EscF = needle protein, rOrf8 = putative inner-rod, EspA/EspD/EspB = translocators, all 'secreted by the LEE-encoded T3SS' in C. rodentium. Core apparatus covered: secretin (EscC), inner-membrane rings (EscJ, EscD), export gate (EscR, EscS, EscT, EscU, EscV), ATPase (EscN), needle (EscF), inner rod (rOrf8/EscI), C-ring (SepQ/EscQ), gatekeeper/secretion switch (SepL, SepD), LEE-essential secretion gene (SepZ), and translocon (EspA, EspD, EspB). Deliberately EXCLUDED as non-machinery: secreted effectors (Tir, EspF, EspG, EspH, Map, EspB's injected activity, and all non-LEE prophage effectors including the curated ROD_06241/06251/07221), the chaperones CesD/CesT/CesF, the intimin adhesin (Eae) and its receptor Tir, and the transcriptional regulator Ler. Lower-confidence calls: SepD/SepL are secretion-control (switch/gatekeeper) proteins rather than structural ring components but are apparatus-associated and SctW/gatekeeper-class per the brief; SepZ has no clean Yersinia structural homolog so its family assignment is tentative; rOrf8 inner-rod role is 'putative' in the source. No EscO/EscL/EscK stalk/stator/C-ring-base genes were separately named with a quote in the C. rodentium-specific sources consulted, so they are omitted rather than padded; the listed set still covers the full secretin-to-translocon core of this injectisome. Did not mine the RefSeq GenBank; ROD locus tags were not assigned. All quotes verbatim from Deng 2001 (Table 3 gene/function pairings) and Deng 2010 (secretome prose).
